eip_status.highlight: EIP-8105 (Universal Encrypted Enshrined Mempool) withdrawn; replaced by Lucid design
New Lucid EIP published (PR #11376); more engineering-specific than research post

protocol_design.highlight: Lucid: two-slot design with tickets reserving block space, keys revealed post-inclusion
Prevents front-running; may reduce back-running efficiency by requiring full top-of-block backrun
Penalty mechanism: 1/64 of top-of-block fee retained if key not revealed
Ciphertext encapsulation uses AEAD per RFC 9180; extensible for post-quantum
Maximum 1/8 block gas for sealed transactions; expandable to 1/2 for catch-up

implementation_requirements.highlight: Requires CL RPC API additions: new event for inclusion notification, key reveal method
PTC votes on key availability; builders must adhere to vote results
Bundling supported: multiple transactions in one commitment with single/multiple keys

ecosystem_considerations.highlight: Interaction with Frames unclear; needs investigation if both in Hegotá
FOCIL interaction: sealed transactions can go in inclusion lists (optional initially)
Optional feature; normal and encrypted transactions coexist; FOCIL prevents censorship

action_items.action: Review Lucid EIP PR #11376 and provide feedback
Engage MEV ecosystem (builders, searchers, wallets) for feedback on Lucid
